title: polybotrya vivipara
intro: Polybotrya vivipara is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, specifically a type of epiphytic fern native to Central and South America. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Polybotrya vivipara include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to create tinctures or salves for topical application.
